excalidraw/excalidraw#2109.body:
Currently, `alt-drag` duplicates in such a way that we swap the original element with a new one (with new seed and shape) instead of the one being dragged. This is undesirable.

:warning: Don't forget to adapt arrow binding as it accounts for current, incorrect behavior, and there are no specs for binding so you won't notice any regressions.
excalidraw/excalidraw#2109.title: alt-drag should not modify original element

excalidraw/excalidraw#7840.body:
## Reproduction

1. Go to https://excalidraw.com
2. Open Library
3. Click vertical "..."
4. Click Open
5. Select any *.excalidrawlib file
6. Same issue with the _Insert Image_ tool when selecting _Choose File_

## Actual behavior
Nothing happens

## Expected  behavior
File loads into the library

## Environment
iOS Version 17.4.1

## PS
It looks like the issue is reproducible only with the most recent iOS update. I have access to a different phone with iOS 17.3.1 where everything worked fine and after upgrading 17.4.1 the issue appeared again.
Looks like only iOS is affected, could not reproduce on latest iPadOS.

While debugging the issue I can see the following warning in the console
Opening the file was canceled (legacy-fs).
Which is coming from the following place

https://github.com/kubiksamek/excalidraw/blob/0820231fc4d17f4ee0eda5d1af5a3f2948c4dff2/src/data/filesystem.ts#L80C25-L80C67
excalidraw/excalidraw#7840.title: Can't open excalidrawlib file or insert image on iPhone with iOS 17.4.1

excalidraw/excalidraw#7921.body:
Local changes within the editor are still being recorded after the `App` component updates. This means that our data flow of capturing changes is `action` -> `componentDidUpdate` -> `store`, but updates through the `updateScene` API end up in the `Store` before component updates - to filter out yet uncommitted local elements and eliminate potential concurrency issues.

As stated in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/7348#discussion_r1516057935, we should not capture the _increments_ post component update, but emit them from the user actions and apply them to the `Store` directly. Additionally, turning on recording in relevant actions and capturing the changes after component updates does not just seem error-prone, but might also lead to unexpected issues related to `React`'s auto-batching & concurrent mode.

Moreover, from the event sourcing perspective, coupling renders with exactly one user action is just plain wrong - we should be able to have multiple actions as part of one render, as well as one "async" action spread across multiple renders.

> In theory, to support async actions properly, we would likely need to establish transactions on the elements level (with transaction id, rollback possibility, etc.)  in case there are other actions triggered in the meantime - such instances are out of the scope of this initiative.

```[tasklist]
### Tasks
- [ ] Remove scheduling of increments capture and snapshot update post component update
- [ ] Identify and refactor actions which are dependent on multiple `setState` calls or are generally asynchronous
- [ ] Update local store data flow from `action` -> `state` -> `componentDidUpdate` -> `store` into `action` -> `store` -> `state`.
```

> Store should not be considered as a mediator of the state, as it is handling only a subset of state operations. Especially it's interested only in (observed) committed data (no ephemerals).
excalidraw/excalidraw#7921.title: Unify `Store` data flow for local updates

excalidraw/excalidraw#8261.body:
Firefox version: 128.0. I don't get the same issue on Chromium 127.0.6533.57.

The problem goes away when I comment [the lines setting `editable.selectionStart` and `editable.selectionEnd`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/df8875a497b2111ec8c21963d66a2e2396d15bbd/packages/excalidraw/element/textWysiwyg.tsx#L223-L224). The problem also goes away if the [`overflow: hidden` line](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/df8875a497b2111ec8c21963d66a2e2396d15bbd/packages/excalidraw/element/textWysiwyg.tsx#L298) is commented.

The following is a video showing the bug in addition to logging the values of `editable.selectionStart` and `editable.selectionEnd` below the mentioned two lines:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/12ad41e1-cb09-4a8d-82dc-62030f468770

This was a bit annoying while taking notes.

I hid the menu at the bottom since it covered the text at the bottom (but that's another issue :sweat_smile:).

The reason the scroll gets reset instead of just not scrolling is because of [the line setting `scrollTop` to 0](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/df8875a497b2111ec8c21963d66a2e2396d15bbd/packages/excalidraw/element/textWysiwyg.tsx#L260). If I comment it, the scrolling just stops.
excalidraw/excalidraw#8261.title: Bug on Firefox: scroll gets reset on appending text before the end of the text

excalidraw/excalidraw#9282.body:
**Description:**
Currently, Excalidraw allows users to draw lines at fixed angles using the **Shift** key (every 15°). However, when modifying an existing line by dragging one of its endpoints, there is no way to constrain the adjustment to maintain the original angle. This makes precise modifications difficult, especially for technical drawings.

**Proposed Feature:**
Introduce a keyboard shortcut that allows users to preserve the original angle of a line when modifying it. For example:
- Holding **Shift** while dragging an endpoint should constrain the movement to maintain the initial angle of the line.
- Alternatively, a combination like **Alt + Shift** or **Ctrl + Shift** could be used if Shift alone is reserved for other functions.

**Benefits:**
- Ensures consistency in structured diagrams.
- Improves usability for users working on precise drawings.
- Aligns with existing behavior of constrained angle drawing but extends it to modifications.

**Possible Implementation:**
- Detect when a user is modifying a line and pressing the defined shortcut.
- Calculate the closest point along the original angle where the endpoint can be repositioned.
- Snap the endpoint accordingly.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/38301399-7e03-4897-9c78-7ac5a2fb3916
excalidraw/excalidraw#9282.title: FR: Maintain Line Angle When Modifying Existing Lines

2) Make use of this new more idiomatically named function by refactoring the files where this formula is used to use the common function. 

Some of the files I found as good candidates for review:
- packages/excalidraw/element/binding.ts
- packages/excalidraw/element/collision.ts
- packages/excalidraw/element/cropElement.ts
- packages/excalidraw/element/distance.ts
...there are certainly more.

excalidraw/excalidraw#9314.body:
When toggling arrow binding, be it by pressing Ctrl when moving an arrow tip, or by using the toggle in Obsidian's comment palette, the other end of the arrow binds itself back to the adjacent shape. I was able to reproduce the issue on Obsidian as well as on the official website.

Here is a screen capture of this issue : 

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/043224e2-9766-4db1-8d26-e6081632a01f

The steps to reproduce are as follows : 

1. Create a new diagram. Draw two rectangles a few units apart. Let's call the first rectangle A and the second B.
2. Draw an arrow from A to B and let it bind to the shapes on both of its ends. Check that the two ends are bound to their respective shapes by moving both shapes around.
3. Unbind the arrow from A by left-clicking and holding its end, dragging it then repositioning it while holding the Ctrl key. Check that this end is not bound to A anymore by moving the A shape around. Then reposition A. Do the same with B to confirm that B is still bound to the other arrow tip.
4. Repeat the same operation on B side : unbind the arrow from B by moving its tip and repositioning it while holding Ctrl. Check that B is not bound anymore to this tip by moving B around.
5. **The other end adjacent to A is now bound to A, instead of staying unbound from step 3**
excalidraw/excalidraw#9314.title: Bug : arrows bind themselves to a shape they were previously unbound of

excalidraw/excalidraw#9462.body:
The new lasso selection seemingly cannot select objects that are outside the original viewport when starting the selection. Scrolling or zooming after the selection was started is ignored.

Demonstration:
1. ✅ Select starting with the middle square and then select all outer ones. No zoom. All squares get selected.
2. ❌ Zoom in. Start the selection with the middle square. Zoom out. Select the outer ones. Only the center square gets selected.
3. ✅ Start selecting outside all squares and select all of them. All squares get selected.
4. ❌ Scroll such that the squares are just out of view. Start the selection. Scroll back. Select the squares. No square gets selected.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/95945c9f-c4df-4736-95b4-0c8af9cdc676
excalidraw/excalidraw#9462.title: Lasso selection breaks when zooming/scrolling

excalidraw/excalidraw#9562.body:
In a Next.js project, Excalidraw does not render correctly out of the box unless its CSS is explicitly imported. This is due to the fact that, unlike some frameworks, Next.js does not automatically include styles from third-party packages unless they are manually imported.

To ensure Excalidraw renders properly, you must add the following import statement in your application (wherever you are dynamically* importing it):

```ts
import '@excalidraw/excalidraw/index.css';
```

Without this import, the Excalidraw canvas will not display or function correctly, as it relies on specific styles for layout and interaction.

Action: Please update the documentation to explicitly mention this requirement for users integrating Excalidraw with Next.js or similar frameworks that do not auto-load package-level CSS.
excalidraw/excalidraw#9562.title: Missing CSS import in documentation for NextJs Integration

excalidraw/excalidraw#9654.body:
With dark mode, using the lasso selection shortcut (alt + drag on Linux) causes flickering.

It seems to be more noticeable after we switch from light to dark (or vice versa) in the same session. So:
- Switch to light mode
- Refresh
- Switch to dark mode
- Try alt + drag

Otherwise, it is just a small canvas reset flash.

Removing `flushSync` fixes it.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7befa11c-aa45-4d78-8b66-3367502a6888
excalidraw/excalidraw#9654.title: Screen flickering with alt lasso selection

excalidraw/excalidraw#9655.body:
The scheduled function via `throttleRAF` currently uses the earliest arguments it receives in a requestAnimationFrame window. This can cause latency in things like freedraw.

https://discord.com/channels/723672430744174682/1375576873303670784/1375582008872538232
excalidraw/excalidraw#9655.title: Make scheduled function via `throttleRAF` use latest arguments in RAF window

excalidraw/excalidraw#9669.body:
### Export-to-Image Preview Flickers When Switching Themes

---

#### Steps to Reproduce:
1. Draw anything using the white color.
2. Open the **Export to Image** modal.
3. Switch between **dark mode** and **light mode**.
4. Observe the preview window.

---

#### Expected Behavior:
The export preview should remain **smooth and stable** when switching themes.

#### Actual Behavior:
The preview **flickers or jitters momentarily** during the theme transition.

---

#### Environment:
- **Browser:** Chrome  
- **Video:**

excalidraw/excalidraw#9850.body:
When creating, pasting, duplicating, or moving elements over an unlocked frame, Excalidraw finds the top layer frame (`getTopLayerFrameAtSceneCoords`) and assigns frameId (via `addElementsToFrame` or direct constructor frameId).

This still occurs even if the frame is locked.

In my view the interaction of a locked frame with its surrounding should be similar to how an arrow interacts with a locked element. If the element is locked, the arrow cannot bind.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ffb43c05-d8f5-4c59-8c94-4e2d8c955f44
excalidraw/excalidraw#9850.title: Locked frames should not auto-add elements (disable automatic frameId assignment)

(I'll copy a huge Excel as in the Obsidian issue example as well) - Paste to Excalidraw - An error message is displayed, and the scene is deleted. https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6447d9f1-13e0-4a55-82ec-143157bf9f17 This is a regression introduced in #9643 (the code on excalidraw.com does not seem to include the changes from 2 months ago yet) Debugging led me to this change as the root cause:

excalidraw/excalidraw#10009.body:
1. install a larger library (20-30 items at least)
2. select all (hold shift and click on first and then the last)
3. drag them quickly over to the canvas

If you're fast enough and the library items are large enough, they won't be inserted on canvas. Also depends on CPU speed.

I haven't checked, but I believe it's because of the delay between starting to drag and how much time the `dataTransfer.setData()` call takes (it's synchronous):

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/f55ecb96cc8db9a2417d48cd8077833c3822d64e/packages/excalidraw/components/LibraryMenuItems.tsx#L176-L184

TODO

- [ ] investigate if this is indeed the root of the problem
- [ ] if so, only encode a lightweight data that serializes quickly (e.g. list of library ids)
- [ ] on drop, parse the ids and resolve to the full library items
excalidraw/excalidraw#10009.title: race conditions with drag-dropping library items on canvas

excalidraw/excalidraw#10033.body:
When you hold shift, you can select multiple library items. Currently this only works in the top-down direction. We should support bottom-top as well.

Make sure that the "holes" are still supported. In other words, make the contiguous selection from _last_ selected item (while keeping previous selection).

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/30c83f73-d611-4479-9a27-29578b0a066c)
excalidraw/excalidraw#10033.title: support bi-directional library item multi-selection

excalidraw/excalidraw#10181.body:
**Description:**

The Text Element written inside a Shape (Rectangle, Diamond, Ellipse) appears to retain a reference or association to the Original Container after being "wrapped" in a New Container using the "Wrap text in a container" action.

This causes the Text Element to be incorrectly associated with two containers simultaneously, leading to errors when manipulating the original object.

Note. The Shapes are: Rectangle, Diamond, or Ellipse.

**Steps to Reproduce:**

- Insert any shape (e.g., Rectangle).
- Write embedded text within the shape (the text is part of the shape, not a separate element).
- Select the shape using the Group Selection action.
- Right-click and select the Wrap text in a container option.
- Ungroup using the Ungroup Selection action.
- Result: There are two separate shapes/containers:
- New Container (small, associated with the text).
- Original Shape (the initial large element).

**The Problem:**

When moving or resizing the Original Shape, the following is observed:

- Movement Issue: When moving the Original Shape, the Text Element inside the New Container also moves with it incorrectly, indicating it still maintains the association to the original parent.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/fc39252b-87c7-4f5b-ab75-1244b7876be5

- Resizing/Editing Issue: When changing the size of the Original Shape or trying to edit the text, the text element appears to interact erratically or visually swap between the Original Shape and the New Container.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/4e2f7c17-5b75-439c-8272-646d38c11c9f


**Desired Behaviour:**

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/dcb97579-48b8-4920-90ee-a3fe69de0fd5
excalidraw/excalidraw#10181.title: [BUG] Text Element incorrectly associated with the original container after "Wrap text in a container"

Shape switcher and other selectors are hidden behind the .mobile-toolbar setting Obsidian's mobile-navbar display:none, reveals the shape switcher. Less likely, but theoretically, there could be an offset on the side as well, in which case the color picker popover is partially blocked.

excalidraw/excalidraw#10528.body:
[Commit 8fb16669ab913b7618c6e55a4aba18275fabd1bb](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/commit/8fb16669ab913b7618c6e55a4aba18275fabd1bb) has introduced a regression on the `examples/with-script-in-browser` application, when running the application, there's an error that blocks the example application from loading.
```
 TypeError: can't access property "svg" of undefined
    <anonymous> renderElement.ts:372
 ```

## How to reproduce

- clone the project
- checkout to 8fb1666 (`git checkout 8fb1666`)
- Run the install and build scripts: `yarn clean-install && yarn rm:build && yarn start:example`
  - Example application at http://localhost:3001 doesn't work
- checkout to the previous commit to `8fb1666`, `f2600fe` ( `git checkout f2600fe`)
- Run the install and build scripts: `yarn clean-install && yarn rm:build && yarn start:example`
  - Example application at http://localhost:3001 works normally

excalidraw/excalidraw#10701.body:
# Description
There appears to be two cases where arrow bindings do not update until the affected elements are manually moved:

## 1. Distribute action on elements with bound elements
- When distribution is applied to elements with arrow(s) bound to them, the arrows do not render in the correct position until the elements are moved.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/15f01820-f249-445f-92f5-1dfae4eb25e6

## 2. When editing bound text exceeds its associated container's height
- When text bound to a container extends beyond the container's height, the associated arrow elements similarly fail to render correctly unless the container is moved.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6b4123f4-d802-46e1-8b13-033e3965fbd2

## Potential fix/areas of interest
For both cases the `updateBoundElements` method seems most applicable.

- The distribute action currently uses `newElementWith`. Historically, this was also used in the align action, but was later replaced with the more current combination of `mutateElement` and `updateBoundElements` methods. (see PR #8998)
- The Plan would be to follow similarly and replace the `newElementWith` to mirror the align implementation.
- For the bound text overflow case, I've located the relevant code path and plan to add `updateBoundElements` after `mutateElement` is applied.

@anviik and I discovered these while looking into a related issue with bound text. We're actively working on a PR to address both these scenarios
excalidraw/excalidraw#10701.title: Bug: 2 scenarios where arrow elements are not updated

excalidraw/excalidraw#10720.body:
Hi Excalidraw team,

I am a developer using Excalidraw via the Obsidian plugin. I've noticed that the current conversion from Mermaid code to Excalidraw elements has a significant limitation regarding **styling**, particularly with **subgraphs**.

### The Problem

When using the native converter, custom styles applied to containers (subgraphs) are currently ignored. While `classDef` for individual nodes works well, the `style` directive for subgraphs is lost during the conversion.

Furthermore, the general mapping of styles from code to canvas is limited, creating "flat" diagrams that require extensive manual editing to leverage Excalidraw’s full potential.

### Use Case & Productivity

I frequently use **AI to generate complex Mermaid code** for educational and architectural purposes. Moving from AI-generated code to a visual canvas is a massive productivity boost, but only if the visual hierarchy and intent (colors, dashed lines, groupings) are preserved.

Improving the parser to handle global styling would allow users to create expressive, information-dense diagrams and mind maps significantly faster, without losing Mermaid's ability to convey meaning through visual styling.

### Reproducible Example

The background colors and border styles for `SG_RED` and `SG_BLUE` are lost when importing this code:

```
graph TD
    %% Node styles work fine
    classDef criticalNode fill:#ff5252,stroke:#b71c1c,color:#fff
    
    subgraph SG_RED ["CRITICAL MODULE"]
        Node01["Important Service"]:::criticalNode
    end

    subgraph SG_BLUE ["DASHED BORDER MODULE"]
        Node02["Feature Module"]
    end

    %% THESE SUBGRAPH STYLES ARE CURRENTLY IGNORED DURING CONVERSION:
    style SG_RED fill:#ffebee,stroke:#c62828,stroke-width:3px
    style SG_BLUE fill:#e3f2fd,stroke:#1565c0,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 10 5

```

### Proposed Improvements

I propose enhancing the mapping logic globally to capture more attributes from the Mermaid parser:

* **Containers/Subgraphs**: Map `fill` to `backgroundColor` and ensure proper z-index layering.
* **Borders**: Map `stroke-dasharray` to `strokeStyle: "dashed"` and `stroke-width` to Excalidraw’s native stroke width.
* **Aesthetics**: Better integration with Excalidraw's styling capabilities, such as fill styles (solid vs. hachure) and opacity.

This enhancement would bridge the gap between structured code and the flexible, high-quality visual thinking that Excalidraw is known for.

Thank you for considering this improvement!
excalidraw/excalidraw#10720.title: Feature Request: Global overhaul of mermaid-to-excalidraw styling (including Subgraph support)

excalidraw/excalidraw#10948.body:
# Description
When a curved Linear Element (non elbow) has a curve which extends past the control points bounding box, and rotation is applied to the linear element. Intersection does not align with the visible rendered linear element. 

By using `debugDrawCubicBezier` in `distanceToLinearOrFreeDraElement` you can see the path for hit detection is offset. This is possibly due to using the wrong rotational center.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ff5a6c9a-74ee-4947-8fd5-a39c99b4dc04

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Draw a curved arrow, where a curve extends past an extreme control point
2. rotate the curved arrow to any non-zero angle
3. Deselect the arrow
4. zoom in and hover over the visually rendered path
excalidraw/excalidraw#10948.title: [Bug] Hit detection on rotated curved arrows

excalidraw/excalidraw#11001.body:
Bug: isLineSegment does not validate second point

File: [packages/math/src/segment.ts](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/packages/math/src/segment.ts)

While going through the isLineSegment implementation, I noticed that it checks segment[0] twice instead of validating both elements of the segment:
```

isPoint(segment[0]) &&
isPoint(segment[0]);
```

Because of this, cases like the following incorrectly pass validation:

`isLineSegment([[0, 0], "invalid"])` // returns true

Ideally, both elements of the segment should be checked:

```
isPoint(segment[0]) &&
isPoint(segment[1]);
```

This can lead to invalid segments being treated as valid, which might cause issues later when segment[1] is used assuming it’s a proper point.
excalidraw/excalidraw#11001.title: math: isLineSegment does not validate second point

import-js/eslint-plugin-import#3185.body:
It's possible to auto-fix detected duplicated import types as inline types with `import/no-duplicates` ("prefer-inline": true), but it's not possible to keep type imports as they are.

Therefore, `import/no-duplicate` should be extended to prefer type imports when fixing duplicates 

❌ Invalid `["error", {"prefer-type-imports": true}]`

```js
import { AValue, type AType, type BType } from './mama-mia'

import { CValue, type CType } from './papa-mia'
```

✅ Valid with `["error", {"prefer-type-imports": true}]`

```js
import { AValue, } from './mama-mia'
import type { AType , BType } from './mama-mia'

import { CValue } from './papa-mia'
import type { CType } from './papa-mia'
```
import-js/eslint-plugin-import#3185.title: Prefer type imports in `import/no-duplicates`

### Describe the bug

1. Set theme/styling to match obisidan
2. Dark theme obsidian
3. dark theme excalidraw
